summary: Performance Optimization Testing - Iteration 279. All 12 features verified: (1) Pipeline page loads with skeleton state, (2) Pipeline hero card renders with school name, signals, CTA, (3) Attention cards render with action labels, (4) Priority/Pipeline toggle works, (5) Journey page loads with progressive loading, (6) Journey hero shows school name, stage, match%, action card, (7) Timeline shows interactions correctly, (8) Sidebar panels load after first paint (coach watch, stability, contacts), (9) Profile ?lite=true excludes photo_url, (10) Navigation Pipeline→Journey→Pipeline works, (11) Backend endpoints respond quickly, (12) Parallel data fetching works.
critical_code_review_comments: PipelinePage.js: useMemo for derived data (lines 171-182) - correctly memoizes archivedPrograms, activePrograms, committedPrograms, allAttention, attentionMap, heroItem - VERIFIED
PipelinePage.js: Skeleton loading state (lines 185-229) - shows skeleton UI while loading=true - VERIFIED

JourneyPage.js: 3-phase progressive loading (lines 240-294) - Phase 1: critical data (program, journey, match-scores), Phase 2: secondary (engagement, gmail, profile?lite=true), Phase 3: background (coach-watch, stability, flags, actions) - VERIFIED
athlete_profile.py: ?lite=true param (line 191-196) - excludes photo_url from response when lite=true - VERIFIED
athlete_dashboard.py: journey endpoint uses asyncio.gather (lines 635-644) - parallel DB queries for interactions, events, athlete - VERIFIED
top_action_engine.py: batch queries (lines 356-439) - compute_all_top_actions uses batch queries instead of N+1 - VERIFIED
startup.py: MongoDB indexes (lines 193-206) - compound indexes for performance on Pipeline & Journey queries - VERIFIED
context_for_next_testing_agent: Performance optimization complete. Key changes: (1) Profile endpoint has ?lite=true param that excludes photo_url (1.3MB→689B), (2) Journey endpoint parallelized with asyncio.gather, (3) Top-actions uses batch queries instead of per-program N+1, (4) MongoDB compound indexes added for Pipeline & Journey queries, (5) Pipeline page has skeleton loading + useMemo for derived data, (6) Journey page has 3-phase progressive loading (critical→secondary→background). All endpoints respond quickly (<1s). Navigation works correctly.

pipeline_skeleton_loading.details: Pipeline page shows skeleton UI while loading, then renders full content

pipeline_hero_card.details: Hero card shows school name (UCLA), action title, 3 signal bullets, CTA button

pipeline_attention_cards.details: Attention cards show action labels (Re-engage Emory now, Follow up with Stanford, Keep connection warm with Florida)

pipeline_toggle.details: Priority/Pipeline toggle works correctly, switches between views

journey_progressive_loading.details: Journey page loads in 3 phases: critical (hero/timeline), secondary (engagement/profile), background (coach-watch/stability)

journey_hero_section.details: Hero shows school name (Stanford), stage (Visit), match% (71%), action card with CTA

journey_timeline.details: Timeline shows 24 interactions with correct structure (id, event_type, type, title, date)

journey_sidebar_panels.details: Sidebar panels load after first paint: Coach Watch (Hot/High confidence), Coaching Stability (Stable), Key Contacts, Communication & Activity

profile_lite_param.details: GET /api/athlete/profile?lite=true excludes photo_url field, smaller response size

navigation_flow.details: Pipeline → Journey (click card) → Back to Pipeline navigation works correctly

backend_response_times.details: Journey endpoint <1s, Engagement endpoint <1s, parallel fetch of 4 endpoints <3s

parallel_data_fetching.details: Pipeline page fetches programs, match-scores, tasks, top-actions in parallel
